Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze an event log containing 5 cases process instances against a provided normative process description I need to identify deviations violations and explain why they are violations based on the text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Second Step Check inventory must happen after Receive order and before payment authorization or fulfillment decisions Path A Sufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Authorize Payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraint 1 Authorize payment after Check inventory and before Package items Constraint 2 Package items after Authorize payment and before Ship order Constraint 3 Ship order after Package items Constraint 4 Close order is the final activity Path B Insufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order Constraint 1 After Check inventory if insufficient go to Cancel order Constraint 2 After Cancel order do not do fulfillment activities Package Ship Constraint 3 Close order follows Cancel order General Constraints Close order must be the last activity in all scenarios Shipping after cancellation is a violation Authorizing payment before checking inventory is a violation Packaging before authorization is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close Comparison Matches Path A exactly Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Comparison Receive Check OK Check Package Violation Text says Package items should only occur after authorization Package Authorize Violation Text says Authorize payment before packaging Close OK Verdict Violation Package before Authorize

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Comparison Receive Check OK Check Authorize OK for Path A but then Authorize Cancel Mixed signals Usually Cancel implies insufficient inventory If inventory was sufficient enough to Authorize why Cancel Doing both is weird

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Cancel Ship Major Violation Text says Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping Close OK Verdict Violation Ship after Cancel

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close Comparison Matches Path B exactly Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Comparison Receive Check OK Check Authorize OK Authorize Ship Violation Text says Package items should occur after authorization and before shipping Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Close OK Verdict Violation Missing Package items before Ship order

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize the Findings Violating Cases 2 3 5 Compliant Cases 1 4

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft the Explanations incorporating textual evidence Case 2 The text states Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging and Package items should only occur after authorization Case 2 packages before authorizing Case 3 The text states Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping Case 3 ships after canceling Also the flow is ambiguous Authorize then Cancel but the Ship after Cancel is the definitive violation Case 5 The text states Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Case 5 skips packaging entirely between Authorization and Shipping

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting In Case 3 is Authorize then Cancel a violation The text says if inventory is sufficient Authorize If insufficient Cancel Doing both suggests a change in status or an error However the prompt explicitly flags shipping after cancellation as a violation I should focus on the clearest violations first The Ship after Cancel is the hard violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Constraints Did I identify which cases Yes 2 3 5 Did I explain why Yes Did I reference the text Yes Did I handle ambiguities Yes interpreted strict sequencing rules
